title: Two Chinese Mathematicians Win 2026 Fields Medal
summary: The International Mathematical Union announced the 2026 Fields Medal winners, awarding four mathematicians including Deng Yu and Wang Hong, the first Chinese nationals to receive the prize. This historic achievement marks a milestone for Chinese mathematics, reflecting the country&\#x27;s growing research strength and potentially inspiring a new generation of mathematicians. Deng Yu was recognized for contributions to partial differential equations, including the rigorous derivation of the Boltzmann equation from hard sphere dynamics. Wang Hong was honored for advancements in harmonic analysis and geometric measure theory, such as progress on the local smoothing conjecture for wave equations.
context: The Fields Medal is awarded every four years to mathematicians under 40 for outstanding achievements, often considered the highest honor in mathematics. Other 2026 winners include John Pardon for symplectic geometry and Jacob Tsimerman for contributions to o-minimality and arithmetic geometry.
